title: Render panel titles in borders
description: Move panel names (e.g., Beads, Details, Status) into the panel border/title area using Textual panel title support, so body space is not consumed by header text lines.
acceptance_criteria: Panel titles are shown in borders rather than as body text headers.
Usable content area increases by removing redundant in-panel header lines.
Regression tests cover title rendering and no loss of existing focus/status cues.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented the bead in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` by removing in-body Beads/Details header lines from the renderer outputs, reclaiming those rows for content, and setting the status panel border title alongside the existing list/detail border titles. Updated tests in `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` to assert titles now live in borders while focus/status cues remain intact, and documented the behavior in `README.md`.
handoff_summary.risks: Low conflict risk: this bead touches shared TUI rendering and nearby tests/docs, so downstream agents changing panel chrome or renderer expectations in the same files should rebase carefully. Validation used targeted `unittest` cases; broader TUI regression coverage was not rerun in this pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Shared TUI files changed: `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` plus adjacent renderer/title tests. Any parallel work modifying panel borders, focus chrome, or renderer output assertions may need merge reconciliation.

conflict_risks: Shared TUI files changed: `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` plus adjacent renderer/title tests. Any parallel work modifying panel borders, focus chrome, or renderer output assertions may need merge reconciliation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
